There are parts this fails to mention. the trade-off for a shield is that it slows you down. your dodge becomes a dodge roll instead of an upright dodge and loses some of its speed and distance. two handed swords and two-handed axes have the same issue. Ace Combat fan ever since Air Combat (also, Electrosphere is the GOAT besides AC4). Even so, I can't get into the absolutely mindbendingly cheesy story world that so many fans have etched into their brains. Seriously, Project Aces writes the same story every game: perfectly good country with perfect princesses and people vs evil country with a fetish for starting wars for no reason. Add a ragtag group of 90s stereotype pilots, mix in some vapid lines about "countries without borders" or "what does the color of the sky mean to you," and you have yourself a totally forgettable Ace Combat story. Oh, and can't forget the cardboard cutout waifus.

Pac Man World Rally's levels should have been the beautiful designs like Frozen Tundra, Lunar Launch, and Rolling Hills Raceway like we saw in all of the cool early screenshots, especially from 2005. What we got instead were redesigns of all levels shown at first, that were bland with smooth, rounded, boring level geometry with none of the thrilling jagged angles, steep track tilts uphill, downhill, or diagonal sideways on curves that the good original unused versions had. The final version also had boring, monotone colors, skyboxes, and almost no texture, everything just felt like light or clay in the shapes of stuff, not textured like the materials they were actually supposed to be, like in the beautiful prototype levels we didn't get. The 2005 beta prototype had such beautiful colors, much better track layouts for all of them that had any layout changes, the textures all felt real and solid, the models were much cooler and more exhilarating to look at, the skyboxes were infinitely better, the hazards to dodge were much cooler, and the levels with different names had much better names. I especially like Lunar Launch, Frozen Tundra, and Rolling Hills Raceway from the 2005 prototype much more in every way than the bland Galactic Outpost, Arctic Iceberg, and Rolling Hills from the final retail game. I know most fans of Pac Man World Rally are upset and feel ripped off that they didn't get the prototype levels in the original, so please PLEASE make Pac Man World Rally Re-Pac as close as conceivably possible to the 2005 beta prototype designs from the early trailers and screenshots instead of the bland 2006 final game designs that everyone got that disappointed at least hundreds of fans. We need the 2005 prototype tracks, or we don't want to buy it. No point if it is just slapping modern graphics on the 2006 version everyone already has. No one wants that, we all want the beautiful prototype levels that we were all so badly upset that we didn't get. Please, Bandai Namco Entertainment, please.

I just hope that when we get Pac Man World Rally Re-Pac that all of the race tracks and battle arenas will be as close of a recreation of the original 2005 prototype designs as possible. I would be extremely upset if it was just giving the blander 2006 final game designs modern graphics. Like, Frozen Tundra was infinitely better than Arctic Iceberg, Lunar Launch was infinitely better than Galactic Outpost, Rolling Hills Raceway was infinitely better than Rolling Hills, and even though Haunted Mansion wasn't that different from Ghost Mansion, all of the differences still looked better. Most of the prototype levels had the same names as the bland final version alternate designs that unfortunately replaced them, but in every case the prototype designs, especially the 2005 ones were trillions times more beautiful in every way, much better colors, textures, lighting, models, and in some cases, the track layouts were different, but always better than the final version, like steep slants uphill, downhill, and to the sides that tilted your car, there were better hazards, like Lunar Launch's laser shooting robot and Molten Mountain's lava geysers that went unused that made the cars smoke. The beautiful open black sky with no tower or rings obstructing the view and the inclusion of the silver Pac Man moon and the orange nebula looked SO MUCH BETTER IN LUNAR LAUNCH than that satellite and orange rings junking up the view with a unrealistic blue/purple with hardly any stars and some being weird colors in the final game that just looked junkie in comparison, in the prototype, the satellite was next to the short cut and only the final jump had a flat target around the entrance, which was much better. I really hate how boring the colors and textures got in the 2006 retail release version of Pac Man World Rally compared to the beautiful 2005 prototype that had such radiant, rich, detailed, solid feeling textures, colors, and geometry. Frozen Tundra used to have you jump next to the yeti, and there were more jump pad texture variations that added excitement to the designs, everything was more detailed in the beautiful prototype that we didn't get and we should also get Subterranean Speedway, Mappy, Mr. Driller, the Frozen Tundra, and Spooky's Castle battle arenas, and the challenge modes in Pac Man World Rally Re-Pac. I have been trying for years to find a playable copy of that prototype, please make Pac Man World Rally Re-Pac based only on its prototype versions of all content, because I am genuinely going to go insane if I don't get to play those versions within my lifetime. I even hate how smooth and rounded everything is in the final game, the banisters in Frozen Tundra's first jump and the jagged sharp looking broken parts of the prack that you used to land on from the jumps looked so cool!

Yo Bandai, what the hell are you even doing?
Your balancing team must be asleep or completely clueless.
You keep over-buffing regen characters like you’re trying to turn the whole game into a brainless healing simulator.
There are dozens of characters in the roster, but only a tiny handful are actually viable.
And surprise, surprise — they’re mostly EX units.
So what is this? A game or just a walking cash grab?
You act like normal characters don’t exist.
Players invest time, grind resources, build units — and you just leave them useless for months.
Every month or two you could easily buff 5–10 regular characters.
But no. You drip-feed balance patches like it physically hurts you to support the full roster.
Stop pretending this is “strategy.”
It’s lazy design and greedy priorities.
If you actually cared about the player base, you’d balance the damn game instead of worshipping EX banners like they’re your only source of oxygen.
Do better.
Because right now, it just looks embarrassing.

Honestly? I don’t understand all this hate towards Little Nightmares 3.

People forget that this is still a continuation of a series that might not have existed at all. Tarsier Studios was acquired and openly said they wanted to create something new, so Supermassive Games took over and did everything to preserve the atmosphere, spirit, and symbolism of the Little Nightmares world. In my opinion, they did a really good job.

Many players complain that the game is short, that there’s no couch co-op, that it feels “different.” But it’s still a solo game, just like the previous entries – the difference is that now you have the choice to play alone or with someone online. Couch co-op would be a nice addition, but it’s not a reason to dismiss the whole game. It’s a feature that could be added later.

People also forget that changing the studio is like asking a modern artist to paint in Leonardo da Vinci’s style. It won’t be an exact copy, but it can still be beautiful in its own way. And that’s exactly what Supermassive did – they didn’t copy Little Nightmares, they gave it new life, keeping the atmosphere while expanding the universe with new ideas like The Sound of Nightmares podcasts, the novella, and the upcoming VR game.

Yes, Little Nightmares 3 may be the weakest game in the series – but that doesn’t mean it’s bad. It just needs a little polishing.

Instead of constantly saying, "Little Nightmares 3 is the weakest game in the series or that ReAnimal is the true continuation," maybe we should appreciate that someone actually took on such a difficult task – recreating something that holds immense emotional value for so many of us.

Supermassive Games didn’t ruin Little Nightmares. They made sure it continues to live.

Guys... I love this game. I really do. I love the fact that my favorite Dragonball game franchise is back after so long, and it's ACTUALLY back, a true, genuine "return to form" we keep hearing about so much lately but rarely ever actually see. 

I'm not unhappy about seeing new characters added by any means, quite the opposite. I'd actually like to see even more of that. But we're still missing a lot of characters from the last installment, and I really hope you haven't forgotten about them, especially the OG Dragonball characters. 

But even more important than that, I REALLY REALLY hope you plan on adding more story mode content. Almost every character except Goku is just missing large chunks of their story for some reason, and only a small handful of characters even have a story mode at all in the first place. I think Bardock could use a story mode at the very least, and I'm honestly surprised he didn't have one this time.

This game could and honestly should eventually become the all encompassing definitive Dragonball fighting game with as many characters and stories as possible. The more "what-if" scenarios, the better.

The first in the series on the PlayStation 2 (the game is called Ace Combat: Distant Thunder in Europe) and a reboot of the series. It depicts the final formation of the Strangereal world and the two-year continental war between ISAF forces and Erusea, a consequence of the impact of the asteroid Ulysses 1994XF04 in 1999. It was the highest-selling game in the series until Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown. It also featured the debut of the new fictional EASA X-02 Wyvern, the availability of the F-15E Strike Eagle, and the debut of the Tornado IDS, the Su-37 "Flanker-F" aka Terminator, and the game's flagship fighter, the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor. Plus, the memorable battle for the Stonehenge installation and the duel between the F-22A "Mobius 1" and the Su-37 "Yellow 13." 

30 years of the Ace Combat series in 2025 and the F-22A Raptor in 2027.

Before they start their live streaming, I want to share my theory with you so you can tell me what do you think about it: 


‼️Spoiler alert: Little nightmares 1 and 3‼️

The boat Low and Alone wake up on is the same boat that transported guests to the Maw in Little Nightmares 1, the sound of which we hear in the first game's post-credits ending when Six leaves the Maw. Additionally, the Dark Six costume set's Nintendo eShop description describes Six as "the girl who escaped the Maw," meaning we now know the theory that Six is the lady or that she stayed in the Maw is wrong.

Now, inside the boat Low wakes up on from his nightmare is a gutted rat, which could suggest that Six had another hunger attack and ate that rat.

Many people have wondered who was piloting said boat and have theorized about it. My theory is that the "guest" we see dead in the middle of the desert at the beginning of Little Nightmares 3 is the one who was piloting it. And why did he die? Was it dehydration or starvation?  It could be, but it's also likely that he died because of Six, since at the end of Little Nightmares 1 we see that Six absorbed the Lady's powers and that she was draining the lives of the guests with that power.

In conclusion, Six escaped the Maw thanks to that ship, then suffered a hunger attack and ate a rat that was nearby, and when the ship stopped, Six (for some reason) killed (accidentally or deliberately) the pilot. So, Six could be in the Spiral, and the reason she won't appear in Little Nightmares 3 is because she's impossible to defeat. Or at least, almost impossible...

Look man, as much as i appreciate the quality of the content of this update, it's the smallest DLC we've gotten so far and is the last CONFIRMED one we're even getting at all.

This game needs so much fundamental improvement and bamco is refusing to give it what it needs.

We need more maps, more characters that have been missing, improved animations for a pretty significant amount of the moves, and overall more polish. although modernized and visually impressive this game still pales in comparison to the depth of BT3 and it's mechanics.

We're still missing destructible stages too, i don't care if there's only one "destroyed earth" and all earth stages when destroyed transition to it (like how BT3 worked) it'd be better than only having a SINGLE destructible stage in the entire game.

Alongside this, tech-wise this game needs a lot, why is there no support for uncapped framerates in offline modes or even online with some simple limitations? For example, you can set a max framerate and when playing online the match is limited to whatever the lowest between the 2 players has at a minimum of 60, there's mods out there that uncap the framerate that prove that it doesn't cause any visual bugs, so what's the issue? also, why does the HDR in this game just make it look worse overall and makes a lot of the ki attacks just look like baked messes?

This game needs a lot of work to measure up to it's predecessor and the standard it set for it's time. sparking zero was going to be THE definitive dragon ball of our time but suffers from some incredibly baseline issues.

The LITTLE NIGHTMARES III demo is live now on PC (STEAM) and will be going live on consoles at 9pm pacific / midnight eastern!
I’m definitely download this demo when I get back online

7/10 Demo, Pretty Decent 

*Spoiler Warning To Those Who Haven’t Played It Yet Or Have Seen A Video*


Pros:
• Co-op was fun (would 100% recommend playing if you can)
• Solo was good
• You see the real world for the first time
• Reasonable character reactions and cute interactions between them
• Good cinematography and cutscenes
• Good atmosphere and sound design 
• Demo is longer, in my opinion, than the Little Nightmares 2 demo
• Actual save spaces?!
• The combat sections are actually fun (especially with co-op)
• When you die while being chased it resets, but if you’re in a safe area it doesn’t reset (with co-op)

Cons: (I will nitpick a bit)
• Hand holding is finicky (with co-op but I don’t know how else they would do it)
• The hand prints on the openable doors (I haven’t checked if it could be disabled yet)
• Some of the cutscenes I feel could be skippable by walking or running instead of locking your controller
• A few lighting errors but thats fine
• When you’re on the zip line you can’t fall if you let go of the holding button
• Low’s bow auto aims at things (but sometimes it doesn’t immediately so I guess it’s balanced? Like I said earlier, I don’t know how else they would have incorporated it)
• Gameplay is what we’ve seen already in previous trailers with a couple extra parts at the end
• At the trapdoor leading to the ending cutscene, it sometimes glitches out (disappearing trapdoor, characters switching spots, and at one point they opened it and they didn’t go through but the cutscene still played without the characters)

But yeah, other than that, pretty solid so far! I’m excited to see how this game will be up on release!

And the ending... figure that one out for yourself, it's INCREDIBLE.

Pinky’s flower in this version has an even number of petals while in the original it was an odd number.

The shadow that scared Clyde originally was a Pac-Man topiary while in this new version is just a shop sign.

The golden fruit is much larger in this game compared to the original, so bulky in fact that Pinky juggled them in a different way!

I hope that when we get Pac Man World 3 Re-Pac that all of Don Bluth's concept art characters get reimplemented into the game, even the ones from the earliest prototype called Pac Man Adventures. The geckos of Gogecka Central, all of the reptiles, dragons, and bird-like characters were excellent! The genetic mutants that Erwin created would be great extra enemies and the big chinned Dig Dug was hilarious and should be used somehow. I love the concept art Spectral Monsters, the way that some of them looked conjoined or had many arms and all of them had translucent skeletons visible under their misty-ness. I also want at least the known removed levels and areas from beta footage in Pac Man World 3 fully restored for its Re-Pac. It'd make it excellent!

- Would recommend playing on hard to get a challenge.
- Game paces tutorials nicely without info dumping.
- Would recommend bumping up the camera sensitivity if playing on controller to get a smooth movement experience.
- Game felt very linear, but that may be the demo

Overall, I would recommend. It's a JRPG that is simple to get into, and does its core mechanics well without overwhelming the player compared to many other games in the genre.

I just hope that the trailer for the second round of Daima is a normal trailer showing adult Vegeta SSJ3 (accessed via skin for Vegeta Z-End), the Tamagamis, Gomah, Kayoshin and Piccolo Mini and Majin Duu (with his "SSJ3" form), but when it gets to the Goku part, Bring me to life by Evanescence starts playing showing every Goku in the game, and when they get to the two SSJ4 versions of Daima (the adult one accessed via Goku skin (End-Z) just like the base Mini accesses his SSJ), the chorus plays. It would be great, and you already used that song with the new Elden Ring, so doing it with Sparking Zero wouldn't be crazy! That song belongs to Dragon Ball in the hearts of the fans.

I don't know if the devs are up to receive some requests, but I'd love to see the Tamagamis (all of them individually) in the next DLC, along with Majin Duu (with his SSJ3 like form maybe? Make his transformation animation a Majin Kuu appearance giving him a cookie please, I beg you), Goku Mini SSJ3, both adult and Mini Goku SSJ4 (make the adult one a skin for the Z-End one we already have that allows him to go SSJ4), same with adult SSJ3 Vegeta (accessible via skin too), the giant Gomah (since we really didn't see anything about the little one, maybe use him in the intro of a battle and that's enough) and, ultimately, please, add Piccolo Mini and Supreme Kai Mini, they're even more relevant than Panzy! That would be 12 characters, one more than the Super Hero DLC! I really love the game, and it has all the keys to become even better. A part from this, I'm only missing more customization options and maps. I've had a blast so far with this game. It has had its issues, but it is still good. I'm only missing more maps, more stage gimmicks like the regular Namek to the destroyed version one (Cell Games Arena and Tournament of Power Stage are in desperate need of that), or the stage change that FighterZ had, that would be dope. A few more skins would be welcome, especially ones like the ones that Super Goku and Android 18 have that give them alternate attacks, like the Goku Mini one! Maybe some from the story mode? The moveset adjustments to keep the canon intact were awesome, and it'd be cool to be able to play them outside episode battles. People are drooling over Gohan Black! I'd also love to see more unique grabs and winning animations. They are so fresh and add a lot to the Dragon Ball experience. I hope they keep adding those with newer updates.
To put an end to my "mucho texto", I loved how they revisited Z Broly's unique grab towards Goku and SSJ3 Goku's intro and charged Ki attack animation, maybe revisit some more camera angles and camera plays in certain attacks to add some impact to it. Also, revisit some facial expressions during the intros and winning animations: as funny as it is seeing Goten smiling while saying "I'M GONNA KILL YOU" to Super Buu, some of those lines could really get some good use of a few adjustments with facial expressions that are already in the game. Overall, I'm happy, but the little details are the ones that we players appreciate. Keep it up, guys!

The most ambitious installment in the series. A futuristic world, a new version of aircraft, with the F-32 at the forefront, and excellent visuals, story, and soundtrack. The Japanese version is excellent, while the export version has been criticized for significantly cutting content.

Twenty-five years after the premiere of AC3, it's clear that the vision for the future has fallen far short. In the real world of 2040, fighters will be manned or optionally manned, with unmanned, loyal wingmen performing most of the missions.

The game's flagship fighter is the Su-37 Super Flanker.

30 years of the Ace Combat series in 2025.

I bought this from a second hand shop back in the 90's here in the UK. I quite liked it and thought of it as a 7/10 game at the time, Good jet fighter game for the playstation. I remember inside the cover was an advert for the 4th game on ps2.... and i didnt buy it. Why? I thought it would be another silent jet fighter game as this one didnt have much of a story. 

Only to realise years later that we had the 'cut down' version of 3, and that the 4th was the start of the 'holy trinity' of ace combat games which i ended up missing out on! 

I bought ace combat assault horizon and didnt think much of it (i hate the us-rus real world stuff i wanted to go back to Strangereal like in 3). Then 7 dropped and yea, ended up going back to other 2nd hand game stores to pick up the holy trinity and complete them! 

I'm really looking forward to the next game and hope they can pull it out of the bag!

Worst game ever made and over hyped.   Clunky and slow everything.   The whole game felt like I had cement boots on, from the ui, to the gameplay, to the scattered story.  I even tried to beef up my character beforehand with guides and this still was one of the worst games I’ve ever played.  Not even the scenery was overly impressive to me. This is definitely a game overhyped for dark souls type fools.  Thankfully I got it years later for $20.  And will sell it for free!  There was no sense of accomplishment, I didn’t feel challenged,  I felt robbed of my time and money.  There are MUCH better fantasy type games to play out there that Elden Ring doesn’t even come close to in terms of fun.  Elden Ring is for suckers.
